title: Workspace Overview
text: This workspace uses three main MySQL schemas. pf_TickleRight_9210 is the tenant business schema and contains contacts, inquiries, followups, members, batches, attendance, invoices, invoice items, payments, branches, venues, services, categories, and reporting metadata. pf_central is the shared platform schema and contains client_db, user, user_bid, user_social, user_permission, client_module, bio_device, request, action, module, and assessment metadata. pf_admin is used mainly for admin-side coupon and advertisement availability logs. Many important read paths use SQL views rather than raw tables, especially invoice_invoiceitem_view, batch_view, session_batch_view, batch_employee_time_view, attendance_cont_view, contact_followup_view, and invoice_invoiceitem_module_view.

title: Contact CRM And Enrollment
text: contact is the main person table. inquiry represents a lead linked to contact through inquiry.contact_id. followup is the reminder and workflow table linked to followup.contact_id and sometimes to parent followups through followup.parent_id. member represents the enrolled state of a contact and is tied to contact through member.contact_id. heard_from is used as the source channel for inquiries, so lead source analytics usually group by inquiry.heard_from joined to heard_from.id. When a user asks for leads, enquiries, source performance, or followup status, the likely core tables are contact, inquiry, followup, heard_from, and member.

title: Batch Session Attendance Domain
text: batch is the class or cohort table. It usually links to category through batch.category_id, branch through batch.bid, venue through batch.venue_id, and level through batch.level_id. batch_time stores recurring schedule details such as batch_id, day_code, start_time, and end_time. batch_employee is the assignment bridge between a batch and the teacher or employee contact, using batch_id and contact_id. session is the dated execution of a class and usually links to batch through session.batch_id and to curriculum through session.session_master_id. attendance connects a contact to a batch or session and is often queried with attendance.contact_id, attendance.batch_id, attendance.session_id, attendance.date, status, negative, bid, and park. When a user asks about classes, teachers, sessions, attendance, absenteeism, or schedules, prefer batch_view, session_batch_view, batch_employee_time_view, or attendance_cont_view if the required fields are already exposed there.

title: Billing Revenue And Package Entitlement
text: invoice is the commercial header and usually links to contact through invoice.contact_id. invoice_item is the sold package or line item and links to invoice through invoice_item.invoice_id, category through invoice_item.category_id, service through invoice_item.service_id, and batch through invoice_item.batch_id. invoice_item also stores start_date, end_date, sessions, sessions_used, discounts, totals, renew, item_change, and park. payment belongs to invoice through payment.invoice_id and usually stores payment amount, date, receipt, pay_mode_text, clear, balance impact, bid, and park. service is a bridge between billing and curriculum and may reference module, topic, and session_master. If a user asks for package usage, expiring packages, invoice totals, payment details, or sold services, invoice_invoiceitem_view is usually the best first query surface.

title: Preferred Query Surfaces
text: Prefer views when they already combine the business context needed by the question. invoice_invoiceitem_view is the best starting point for invoice, package, member, branch, batch, and service questions. invoice_invoiceitem_module_view is the best starting point for module-level coverage inside sold packages. batch_view is the best starting point for batch schedule and assigned employee display. session_batch_view is the best starting point for date-specific sessions with module and teacher context. batch_employee_time_view is useful for recurring batch schedules with teacher assignments and possible session overrides. attendance_cont_view is useful for attendance reports because it enriches attendance with contact, batch, session, and module context. contact_followup_view is useful for CRM timeline and followup analysis because it resolves followup master_data from inquiry, invoice item, or payment context.

title: Central Identity And Permission Model
text: pf_central.client_db maps tenant database names to client ids. pf_central.user is the main platform login table and is linked to client_db through user.client_id. user often links back to tenant contact through user.contact_id. user_bid gives branch-level access, user_social stores email or social identity, user_permission links a user to a client_module through user_permission.cm_id, and client_module links a client to an enabled module through client_module.client_id and client_module.module_id. When a user asks which users can access a branch, which modules are enabled for a client, or how an employee account maps to a tenant contact, query pf_central.user, user_bid, user_social, user_permission, client_module, and client_db.

title: Request Queue And Automation
text: pf_central.request is a queue or workflow table, not a simple relational child table. It stores action_id, client_id, table_name, row_id, date, time, and links to queued email or SMS data. request.table_name plus request.row_id is a polymorphic application reference. It can point to business objects such as session_batch_view or invoice_invoiceitem_view. action is the catalog behind request.action_id. When a user asks about scheduled messages, queued actions, reminders, SMS jobs, or email jobs, query pf_central.request together with action, email_ready, or sms_ready and do not assume table_name plus row_id is a formal foreign key.

title: Branch Venue And Settings Model
text: branch is the main operational partition inside a tenant database. Many runtime settings are scoped by branch bid. attendance_settings, invoice_settings, and payment_settings all behave like branch-scoped configuration tables through their bid column. venue is mainly used for offline operations, venue rent, classroom mapping, and venue-based revenue logic. batch.venue_id is used widely in code and reporting even if not always enforced as a foreign key. When a user asks about branch-level invoice numbering, attendance rules, payment merchant settings, or venue-based reporting, include branch, venue, attendance_settings, invoice_settings, and payment_settings.

title: Default Filters And Flags
text: Most operational queries should filter out parked or inactive rows. Common defaults are park = 0 on contact, inquiry, followup, member, batch, invoice, invoice_item, payment, and many views. Invoice and member questions may also require invoice.dropout = 0 or invoice.freeze = 0 depending on whether the user wants active commercial state. Attendance and session questions often need date filters and branch filters using bid. Invoice item entitlement logic often depends on start_date, end_date, sessions, and sessions_used. If the natural language request says active, valid, current, open, or ongoing, prefer current-date filters and exclude parked, dropped out, or frozen records unless the request explicitly asks for archived or trashed data.

title: Business Synonyms To Schema Terms
text: Map natural language to schema terms carefully. lead, enquiry, prospect usually means inquiry plus contact. student, child, enrolled member usually means member plus contact. trainer, teacher, tutor usually means employee or emp_cont_view and may be attached to a batch through batch_employee or session_batch_view.contact_id. class, batch, program group usually means batch. class session, session, class date usually means session or session_batch_view. package, plan, course purchase usually means invoice_item joined to invoice and service. receipt or payment entry usually means payment. source channel or heard from usually means inquiry.heard_from joined to heard_from. branch, center, location usually means branch, while venue is usually the physical offline location inside or under a branch context.

title: Common Join Recipes
text: Use these recurring join patterns. For package or invoice questions, join invoice to invoice_item on invoice.id = invoice_item.invoice_id, then join contact on invoice.contact_id = contact.id, join service on invoice_item.service_id = service.id, join category on invoice_item.category_id = category.id, and left join batch on invoice_item.batch_id = batch.id. For member status questions, join member to contact on member.contact_id = contact.id and often join invoice on member.contact_id = invoice.contact_id. For attendance questions, join attendance to contact on attendance.contact_id = contact.id and to batch on attendance.batch_id = batch.id, or use attendance_cont_view. For schedule questions, join batch to batch_time on batch.id = batch_time.batch_id and to batch_employee on batch.id = batch_employee.batch_id, or use batch_view or batch_employee_time_view. For user identity questions, join pf_central.user to client_db on user.client_id = client_db.id and join user_bid or user_permission as needed.

title: NL To SQL Guardrails
text: Do not invent foreign keys just because columns have matching names. Prefer view-defined and application-observed joins from the workspace docs. Prefer tenant views when they already contain the requested business meaning. Qualify schema names when mixing pf_TickleRight_9210 with pf_central or pf_admin. If a question is ambiguous between contact and user, remember that contact is the tenant person record and user is the platform login record. If a question asks for business facts like active package, expiring member, branch revenue, or teacher schedule, it is usually better to query an enriched view than to reconstruct the logic from raw tables. If the question asks for configuration, permissions, or queue state, query raw central tables directly.

title: Example NL Intent Routing
text: Examples: 'Show active packages expiring this month by branch' should start from invoice_invoiceitem_view with park filters, date filters on end_date, and grouping by bid or branch. 'Show teacher schedule for tomorrow' should start from session_batch_view for date-specific execution or batch_employee_time_view for recurring schedules. 'Show leads by source for this week' should start from inquiry joined to heard_from or from an inquiry reporting view if one is available. 'Show payment details for an invoice number' should start from invoice joined to payment or use invoice_invoiceitem_view plus payment. 'Which users have access to branch 27 and module X' should start from pf_central.user joined to user_bid, user_permission, client_module, and module.
